description_long: This is a handheld two-way radio transceiver, commonly known as a walkie-talkie, with a rectangular black body and a prominent antenna extending from the top. The front face features a speaker grille, a push-to-talk button, and the brand name 'KENWOOD' clearly visible above the speaker. The sides of the device display several physical buttons and a rotary knob, likely for volume and channel selection, as well as a belt clip attachment on the back. The rear side includes a battery compartment with product information labels and a visible latch for battery removal. The device is constructed primarily from durable plastic with some metallic components for internal structure and connectors. Its compact, rugged design is suitable for portable use in urban environments by security personnel, event staff, or maintenance workers.
description: A black handheld Kenwood walkie-talkie with a long antenna, speaker grille, and physical control buttons, designed for portable communication.
description_view_0: The left side view shows a side button and a rotary knob, with minimal surface detail visible.
description_view_1: The front view displays the Kenwood logo, speaker grille, and main control interface, including the push-to-talk button.
description_view_2: The right side view highlights additional buttons and a side port, with a slim profile.
description_view_3: The rear view reveals the battery compartment, product labels, and a belt clip attachment.
